reasoning: The position is the product of velocity and time, with an initial offset of the initial position. Since the velocity and time are given, we can find the initial position by subtracting the position from the product of velocity and time.

To find the initial position, we can rearrange the position equation (position = initial_position + velocity * time) to isolate the initial position. Subtracting the current position from the product of velocity and time gives the initial position. However, we cannot manipulate the position directly. Instead, we will adjust the initial_position to match the calculated value.
